steps.title: Identify the structures at the boundary
steps.content: Left side: independent clause with a relative clause needing an object (“who served in” needs completion). Right side: noun phrase with no verb.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon introduces a list, elaboration, or clarification that follows a complete statement. It signals that the names of the two Americans follow as specific examples. A period would create a sentence fragment; a semicolon requires an independent clause on the second side, which this noun phrase is not; and no punctuation creates an awkward construction. That matches choice B — B is correct.
